title: helianthemum echioides
intro: Helianthemum echioides, also known as rockrose or frostweed, is a perennial flowering plant native to North America. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The plant's health benefits include soothing skin irritations, reducing fever, and promoting wound healing. The herbal preparations of Helianthemum echioides include teas, infusions, and salves, which can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water or oil to extract its medicinal compounds.
